rationale.R1.2: The section defines interpretation alignment as matching individual user intent, then operationalizes it via a majority-vote label—a logical tension that may be load-bearing depending on whether critique or polish is requested.
rationale.R1.3: The accuracy gap (71.8% vs 58.4/64.1%) is called 'direct evidence' of user-intent alignment, but majority-vote labels are a corpus-level proxy, not individual-intent ground truth; this evidence-claim gap is a plausible central concern.
